title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
summary: AI engineers at frontier labs use self-prompting loops where an orchestrator agent spawns worker agents in parallel rather than prompting models directly. This approach scales task execution but requires observability tooling and incurs higher token costs as loops run for extended periods.
writeup: Cole Medin examines a workflow used by AI engineers at frontier labs in which an orchestrator agent spawns worker agents in parallel instead of an engineer prompting models directly, a pattern described as self-prompting loops. The shift matters because it scales task execution across many agents running for extended periods. The concrete tradeoffs: the approach depends on observability tooling to track what the agents do, and it incurs higher token costs as the loops run.
